new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverage obtained exclusively by alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries (e.g. apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, blackcurrants, whitecurrants, redcurrants, strawberries, apples), classified under CN subheading 2208 90 48 (Chapter 22).

new_classes.label_en: Other Fruit Fermentation and Distillation Process
new_classes.label_de: Gärungs- und Destillationsprozess aus anderen Früchten
new_classes.definition_en: Process of alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries (e.g. apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, currants, strawberries, apples) to produce other fruit spirits; used to discriminate CN subheading 2208 90 48 from the plum/pear/cherry brandy subheadings 2208 90 33 and 2208 90 38.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the main distinguishing criteria for CN 22089048: (1) it is a spirit (subclass of eucn:Spirit), (2) produced exclusively by alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit (hasFruitDistillationProcess some OtherFruitFermentationDistillation), (3) the fruit is explicitly NOT plums, pears or cherries (complement restriction vis-à-vis PrunePearCherryBrandy and PrunePearCherryFermentationDistillation), and (4) the CN code is pinned via cnHeadingCode hasValue. A minor gap remains: the legal text also implies container-size indifference at this level (2208 90 48 is not split by container size in this subheading) — no container volume restriction is modelled, which slightly reduces coverage. Additionally, the exhaustive list of example fruits (apricots, bilberries, raspberries, etc.) is not individually represented in the ontology, which is by design (examples are non-exhaustive). The 'exclusively' qualifier of the production process is captured through the process class definition but cannot be fully formalised in OWL-DL without n-ary constructs.
